reasoning: As of October 26, 2025, there is no credible evidence suggesting that Donald Trump will be removed via the 25th Amendment by the end of the year. Recent medical evaluations from October 11, 2025, describe him as being in 'exceptional health' and 'fully fit.' Despite political calls from Democratic leaders like Governor JB Pritzker in early October to invoke the 25th Amendment following controversial remarks about using the military in U.S. cities, reporting indicates that Trump retains the 'ironclad allegiance' of his Cabinet and Vice President JD Vance. Rumors suggesting JD Vance was lobbying the Cabinet for such a move have been debunked as satire from 'The Halfway Post.' Given that Section 4 of the 25th Amendment requires the Vice President and a majority of the Cabinet to initiate the process—and would likely face an insurmountably high bar of 2/3 approval in both houses of Congress if contested—the event is highly improbable barring an unforeseen and catastrophic medical emergency in the final two months of 2025.
